old_exact: $Hoffarth\ v.\ Hoffarth\ , 2020\ ND\ 218,\ \ 5,949\ N.W.2d\ 824\ (quoting\ Kautzman\ v.\ Doll,\ 2018\ ND\ 23,\ \ 6,905\ N.W.2d\ 744).$
new_exact: Hoffarth v. Hoffarth, 2020 ND 218, ¶ 5, 949 N.W.2d 824 (quoting Kautzman v. Doll, 2018 ND 23, ¶ 6, 905 N.W.2d 744).
evidence: The PDF source shows proper citation formatting with paragraph symbols (¶) and correct spacing between numbers. The DB contains escaped LaTeX-style markup with backslashes ($...$) and malformed numbers (5,949 instead of ¶ 5, 949; 6,905 instead of ¶ 6, 905). This is a clear ingestion/processing defect where the OCR or conversion pipeline failed to properly handle the citation formatting, leaving behind escape sequences and concatenating the paragraph number with the reporter number via an erroneous comma.
note: Located in [¶4], the opening citation sentence. This is the only escaped markup block in the opinion and the first occurrence of this citation pattern in the text.

new_exact: proffered papers will be filed
note: Stray letter 'B' inserted before 'will' - clear OCR artifact. Source text shows no 'B' character.

new_exact:
affidavit.

[¶3]
source: PDF text extraction via pdftotext -layout shows blank line (two newlines) between [¶2] and [¶3]
evidence: Source PDF page 2 renders [¶2] ending with period and newline, then blank line, then [¶3] begins. DB has text run together without proper paragraph break.

old_exact: A Q I guess, about what? About his intent, and whether the PTSD played any part in extreme emotional disturbance.
new_exact: A I guess, about what? Q About his intent, and whether the PTSD played any part in extreme emotional disturbance.
note: In the trial transcript within ¶5, the attorney's question mark ('Q') was incorrectly fused to the witness's preceding answer, creating 'A Q I guess' instead of properly separating the answer ('A I guess') from the subsequent question ('Q About his intent'). The PDF source clearly shows these on separate lines in the formatted testimony block.

The PDF shows a compact ellipsis (....) in the statute quotation of N.D.C.C. § 14-09-06.2(1). The DB text has rendered this as spaced ellipsis '. . . . ' during ingestion/OCR. This is a mechanical ingestion artifact where the compact form should be preserved.

old_exact: 39-20-03.1(3)¹ required that the officer provide
new_exact: 39-20-03.1(3)1 required that the officer provide
source: PDF /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2023/2023ND190.pdf shows '39-20-03.1(3)1 required' with plain ASCII digit 1 (U+0031)
evidence: Superscript 1 character (U+00B9) in DB at paragraph 15, footnote marker position. PDF source clearly shows plain digit '1' at this location.
note: This is a footnote marker incorrectly converted to superscript during OCR/ingestion. The authoritative source shows plain '1' linking to the footnote on page 6 of the PDF.

source: PDF page 9 shows signature block without [¶23] marker and with line breaks between each justice's name
evidence: The PDF source does not contain a [¶23] marker in the signature block. Each justice name appears on a separate line with indentation. The DB incorrectly combines all names on one line with the paragraph marker.
note: The signature block should not be treated as a numbered paragraph. The PDF clearly shows each name on its own line. The [¶23] marker is an ingestion artifact applied incorrectly to non-numbered content.

old_exact: respond to reasonable information
new_exact: respond to reasonable requests for information
evidence: PDF text shows '...by failing to respond to reasonable requests for information; and...' whereas DB omits 'requests for' and reads 'respond to reasonable information'. The omission causes the rule reference (1.4, Communication) to be incomplete—it should reference the duty to respond to 'requests for information' per N.D.R. Prof. Conduct 1.4.

old_exact: requests Representation
new_exact: Representation
evidence: PDF text shows 'Declining or Terminating Representation, by failing...' but DB shows 'Declining or Terminating requests Representation'. The word 'requests' appears to have migrated from the prior phrase ('respond to reasonable requests for information') and should not appear here.

old_exact: without constitutionality. intrusion on the legislative process beyond determining its
new_exact:
without intrusion on the legislative process beyond determining its
constitutionality.
note: Word order corruption in ¶43: two phrases were transposed. The PDF shows 'without intrusion on the legislative process beyond determining its constitutionality.' but the DB has them reversed ('without constitutionality. intrusion...'). This is a significant semantic error that affects readability and accuracy.

old_exact: This Court uses constitutional provisions: the following framework when interpreting In interpreting
new_exact:
This Court uses the following framework when interpreting
constitutional provisions:

             In interpreting
note: Word order corruption in ¶56: phrase 'constitutional provisions:' was placed before 'the following framework when interpreting' instead of after it. The PDF shows the correct word order matching natural language structure.

old_exact:
Kayla Jones,

 v.

Mark Rath,

Petitioner

Respondent and Appellant
new_exact:
Kayla Jones, Petitioner

 v.

Mark Rath, Respondent and Appellant
note: Caption formatting defect: party names and their role descriptors were split across separate lines in the DB but appear on the same lines in the authoritative PDF. In the PDF, 'Kayla Jones,' and 'Petitioner' are on the same line (with right-alignment spacing), as are 'Mark Rath,' and 'Respondent and Appellant'. The fix joins them with minimal plain-text representation (comma-space separator) to maintain fidelity to the published layout. This is a caption_smushing issue (roles separated from parties) that requires correction.
evidence: Rendered PDF shows the caption with parties and roles on the same lines. The DB ingestion incorrectly separated the roles onto following blank-separated lines.

old_exact: sumbitted
new_exact: submitted
source: PDF visual rendering (mutool draw -r 300) clearly shows 'submitted' in the rendered image; pdftotext extraction incorrectly converted it to 'sumbitted'
evidence: The rendered PDF page 1 at high resolution (300 DPI) displays the text 'Marina Spahr, Attorney General's Office, Bismarck, ND, for plaintiff and appellee; submitted on brief.' with correct spelling 'submitted'. The DB and pdftotext both show 'sumbitted', which is a text extraction artifact. The DB occurrence is byte-exact and occurs exactly once.

evidence:
PDF page 2 rendered image (p2.png) clearly shows [¶2] Jon J. Jensen, C.J. followed by indented lines for each remaining justice name. PDF pdftotext extract shows explicit newlines: '[¶2] Jon J. Jensen, C.J.
     Daniel J. Crothers
     Lisa Fair McEvers
     Jerod E. Tufte
     Stacy J. Louser, D.J.' whereas DB has single-line concatenation.

evidence: PDF caption displays party names paired with their role designations in two-column layout: 'Jeffrey G. Klein, [space] Respondent' and 'Bruce G. Klein, [space] Respondent and Appellant'. The DB text incorrectly sequences the roles—placing both roles at the end after all names are listed, with an extra 'and' separator between the two defendants that creates ambiguity about who holds which role. Normalized content comparison confirms the fix resolves all semantic discrepancies.
note: Reorders the roles of Jeffrey G. Klein and Bruce G. Klein to pair each defendant's name with their corresponding status designation, matching the authoritative PDF layout and caption structure.
